title: primula tschuktschorum subsp. arctica
intro: Primula tschuktschorum subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae, native to the Arctic regions of Russia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Primula tschuktschorum subsp. arctica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
